summary: The infant likely has Neonatal Respiratory Distress Syndrome due to its typical presentation after discharge from hospital, aligning well with sudden onset of severe breathing issues post-stable condition over recent days; treatment approaches like surfactants are consistent with managing such cases effectively. Agent2 concludes it’s more plausible than other conditions discussed based primarily on timing relative to previous stability followed by acute decline. To confirm further diagnostic testing or imaging may still be warranted despite strong suspicion towards NRDS at current stage.
